axis: bridge / alert subscription on atlas time-series
title: atlas_alert_subscriber — JSONL stream alerter on bridge_anomaly verdicts
summary: Build a CLI-only, sweeper-safe .hexa subscriber that polls state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl (the sibling JSONL produced by ω-bridge-16), filters scope=bridge_anomaly, tails the last N rows, and evaluates two configurable ratio rules: WARN-density and INSUFFICIENT_DATA-density. Each fired rule emits a positional sentinel; absence of fires emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ PASS. Read-only on the timeline file (raw 71/77) and tolerates parallel-session appends because read_file is a snapshot syscall.

predecessors.omega_bridge_14: introduced the bridge_anomaly analyzer (.hexa) + history TSV schema
predecessors.omega_bridge_15: wired bridge_health.sh to append per-bridge rows + opt-in anomaly auto-invoke
predecessors.omega_bridge_16: promoted __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ sentinel into a structured sibling JSONL row scoped 'bridge_anomaly'

context.problem: Time-series sibling rows existed but no consumer reasoned over verdict trends. A single-sweep WARN is just a snapshot; operators need rate-of-fire signals (e.g. half the last 10 sweeps WARNed) and meta-signals (history isn't accumulating — too many INSUFFICIENT_DATA verdicts in a row).
context.design_goal: One-shot poll (NOT continuous tail -F — kept simple for stage1). Read up to current EOF. Configurable rules via HEXA_ARGV. CLI-only invocation via `hexa run`. No library/path coupling to other tools.

sentinels: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=warn_ratio ratio=R threshold=T last_n=N sampled=S
__ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=insufficient_ratio ratio=R threshold=T last_n=N sampled=S
argv_flags: --timeline PATH (override; for fixtures + future remote consumers)
algorithm: 2. Read timeline; filter rows containing scope marker '"scope":"bridge_anomaly"'
4. Tail to last N via shell (avoids hexa int slice arithmetic)
5. Tally PASS/WARN/INSUFFICIENT_DATA by substring presence on '"verdict":"..."' marker
6. Single awk call computes both ratios formatted to 4 decimals (avoids hexa float parse)
7. Two awk >= comparisons evaluate each rule

validation.observed: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=warn_ratio ratio=0.5000 threshold=0.4 last_n=10 sampled=10
__ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=insufficient_ratio ratio=0.8000 threshold=0.6 last_n=10 sampled=10

validation.edge_case: live nexus timeline (no bridge_anomaly rows yet)
custom threshold raised above observed ratio

validation.observed: tail of 5 rows = 2 PASS + 3 WARN; warn_ratio=0.6 fires
single-line sentinel output, no human summary
usage line + flag descriptions printed; tool returns without scanning

scope_constraints_honoured: raw 71 read-only — never writes to atlas_health_timeline.jsonl (parallel-session-safe)
raw 73 admissibility — schema parsed by stable substring markers (single-producer guarantee from ω-bridge-16)
raw 77 append-only-respect — does not edit the timeline at all
raw 80 sentinel — every code path emits exactly one __ATLAS_ALERT__ line family
CLI-only inter-call — invokable via `hexa run tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa`, no library/path coupling

no parallel-session collision — only created NEW file; did not touch cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked

non_changes.atlas_health_timeline.jsonl: currently dirty (parallel-session); subscriber is read-only and tolerates concurrent appends because read_file is a single-syscall snapshot
non_changes.bridge_anomaly.hexa: untouched — analyzer remains source of truth
non_changes.bridge_health.sh: untouched — ω-bridge-16 already wired the sibling JSONL row

next_followups: tail -F mode (continuous subscription) for live operator dashboards
additional rule kinds: flag-spike-rate (sum of 'flagged' over last N >= K) and verdict-flap (PASS↔WARN oscillation count)
alert quench / cooldown so a sustained outage doesn't re-fire on every sweep
downstream sink: route FIRED sentinels into a separate alerts JSONL (not the canonical timeline) for audit retention
